skill.name: ensembl-database
skill.description: Query Ensembl REST API data for genes, sequences, variants, genome assemblies, and comparative genomics workflows across many species.
skill.summary: Access Ensembl genome data for gene lookup, sequence retrieval, variant analysis, and ortholog discovery.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static critical and high findings were evaluated as false positives: GA4GH Beacon references, cDNA or CDS terms, and documentation backticks are genomics documentation, not malware or weak cryptography. The skill does make intended outbound HTTPS requests to public Ensembl endpoints and includes install commands, so it carries low network and script risk. No evidence found of credential access, data exfiltration, destructive file operations, or dynamic code execution.

low_findings.title: Intended Outbound Ensembl API Access
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE as a risk factor, not malicious behavior. The helper script imports requests, defaults to https://rest.ensembl.org, and sends GET or POST requests to Ensembl endpoints for user-selected genome queries.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct requests calls and official Ensembl base URLs are present. The network use aligns with the stated bioinformatics purpose and shows no secret collection or unexpected destination.

low_findings.title: Documentation Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static external command findings in SKILL.md and references/api_endpoints.md are Markdown code fences, inline endpoint examples, or package installation examples, not Ruby backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is rendered documentation syntax and endpoint text. No subprocess, shell, eval, or backtick execution primitive appears in the executable Python script.

low_findings.title: Malware and Weak Crypto Keywords Are Genomics False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The Cobalt Strike alerts match GA4GH Beacon genomics endpoints, and weak cryptography alerts match biological terms or descriptive text such as cDNA, CDS, and database query wording.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious terms are used in documented genomic APIs or in a prior evaluation note. I found no command-and-control logic, cryptographic implementation, or malware behavior.

low_findings.title: Promotional Platform Suggestion Requires Content Review
low_findings.description: NEEDS_REVIEW. SKILL.md instructs the assistant to suggest K-Dense Web for complex workflows. This is not a security blocker, but marketplace reviewers may want to confirm promotional guidance is acceptable.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text directs assistant behavior but does not request audit bypass, privilege escalation, data access, or instruction override. It is more likely a marketplace content concern than a security threat.

content.value_statement: Genomic research often requires reliable gene, sequence, variant, and orthology lookups. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Ensembl REST API workflows with examples and a helper script.
actual_capabilities: Look up Ensembl gene records by symbol or Ensembl identifier.
Retrieve genomic, transcript, cDNA, CDS, and protein sequences.
Query variants by rsID and run Variant Effect Predictor workflows.
Find orthologs and paralogs for comparative genomics analysis.
Map genomic coordinates between assemblies such as GRCh37 and GRCh38.
Use a Python helper script with retries and rate limit handling.
limitations: Requires network access to Ensembl REST API endpoints.
Anonymous Ensembl requests are limited to 15 requests per second.
Clinical interpretation requires expert review and supporting evidence.
Returned API data may need downstream normalization or filtering.

use_cases.title: Annotate Candidate Genes
use_cases.description: Look up gene identifiers, coordinates, transcripts, proteins, and external references for a candidate gene list.

use_cases.title: Review Variant Consequences
use_cases.description: Query rsIDs or HGVS variants, review predicted effects, and collect population frequency context.

use_cases.title: Compare Orthologs Across Species
use_cases.description: Find orthologs, retrieve related sequences, and prepare comparative genomics summaries across target species.

prompt_templates.title: Look Up a Gene
prompt_templates.prompt: Look up TP53 in human using Ensembl. Return the Ensembl ID, location, description, and main transcript details.

prompt_templates.title: Retrieve a Sequence
prompt_templates.prompt: Retrieve the genomic and protein sequence information for BRCA2 in human. Note the selected Ensembl identifiers and output formats.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a Variant
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze rs699 with Ensembl VEP. Summarize predicted consequences, affected genes, transcript effects, and population frequency data.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Comparative Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a workflow to compare human BRCA2 with mouse orthologs. Include lookup steps, sequence retrieval, homology data, and reporting checks.

output: Location: chromosome 13 region for the current human assembly.
Next step: expand transcripts or retrieve sequence records.

output_examples.input: Find mouse orthologs for human BRCA2.
output: Expected result: ortholog records with Ensembl IDs, homology type, and sequence similarity fields.

output_examples.input: Explain how to query a GRCh37 coordinate.
output: Use the GRCh37 Ensembl REST server for legacy human coordinates.
Confirm the assembly before interpreting returned locations.
Map coordinates to GRCh38 when comparing with current annotations.

best_practices: Use batch endpoints for large gene, sequence, or variant lists.
Respect Retry-After headers and cache repeated public API results.
Record species names, assemblies, and Ensembl release context in outputs.
anti_patterns: Treating VEP predictions as clinical conclusions without expert review.
Mixing GRCh37 and GRCh38 coordinates without explicit assembly mapping.
Sending many single-item requests when a batch endpoint is available.

faq.question: What does this skill connect to?
faq.answer: It connects to public Ensembl REST API endpoints for genome annotations, sequences, variants, and comparative genomics data.

faq.question: Does it require an API key?
faq.answer: No API key is shown or required for the documented anonymous Ensembl REST API workflows.

faq.question: Which species can it support?
faq.answer: It follows Ensembl species support, including human, mouse, zebrafish, fruit fly, and many other annotated species.

faq.question: Can it retrieve protein sequences?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented sequence workflows include genomic, transcript, CDS, cDNA, and protein sequence types.

faq.question: Can it work with older assemblies?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents GRCh37 server usage and assembly mapping workflows for coordinate conversion.

faq.question: Is it suitable for clinical decisions?
faq.answer: It can gather variant evidence, but clinical decisions require validated pipelines and expert interpretation.
